name: auth-is-header-based-pat
hook: Dashboard auth is header-based PAT (localStorage → x-kody-token) — no server-side session, no OAuth flow, no JWT.
body:
Authentication on the Kody Dashboard is **entirely header-based**: the user's GitHub PAT lives in `localStorage` as `kody_auth` and is sent on every API call as the `x-kody-token` header. The server resolves the identity via `/api/kody/auth/me` → GitHub `/user`. There is no session cookie, no OAuth callback, no JWT. Source: `src/dashboard/lib/hooks/useGitHubIdentity.ts` (`@ai-summary` comment).

**Why:** Older docs (README, AGENTS, DEPLOY) and many code comments still reference the old OAuth flow. The OAuth flow was removed; the docs were never fully updated. This causes churn on every auth-touching chore (you have to keep re-asserting "this is stale, don't trust it").

**How to apply:** When working on anything auth-related, treat any reference to "OAuth", `/api/oauth/`, "OAuth callback URL", "OAuth scopes", or "session JWT" as suspect. The truth lives in `useGitHubIdentity.ts` and the App-installation-token helpers in `src/dashboard/lib/auth/`. The KODY_MASTER_KEY powers vault AES + chat-ingest HMAC only — not session JWT. If a doc or comment says otherwise, it is stale.
why: Stale auth references in user-facing docs are a recurring source of confusion; having a single source of truth prevents the next chore from re-discovering the same drift.

name: verify-gate-fails-on-prettier-drift-not-just-changed-files
hook: The verify gate runs prettier --check on the whole tree — pre-existing format drift on main will fail the gate even if the chore's own files are clean. Plan for a format sweep if format drift is likely.
body:
The Kody `verify` tool runs `prettier --check "**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx,json,md,yml,yaml}"` against the entire repo, not just files touched by the chore. If `main` has any pre-existing format drift (currently ~70 files), the verify gate fails and the chore is re-invoked with a retry, even when the chore's own changes are format-clean.

**Why:** Hit on issue #322 — docs chore edited 3 files, all passed prettier, but the gate still failed on 70 unrelated files. The previous run's handoff noted "pre-existing in unrelated files, out of scope" — correct per the chore contract, but the gate doesn't care about chore scope.

**How to apply:** (1) Before declaring success on a chore, run the full `prettier --check` (not just the changed files) to see if a sweep will be needed. (2) If format drift is likely, budget for `npx prettier --write` on the failing files in the same PR — note explicitly in the handoff that this is a format-only sweep, not feature work. (3) Strongly consider a followup to add `prettier --check` to the husky pre-commit hook so drift can't reach `main`.
why: Format drift on main is a known recurring failure mode for this project's verify gate; a future chore agent shouldn't have to rediscover this from a verify-failed retry message.
